entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L). Millsp., a legume crop.

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The condition where a plant is unable to produce functional pollen.

entities.name: FEMALE FERTILITY
entities.description: The condition where a plant is able to produce functional ovules and seeds.

entities.name: TRANSLUCENT ANTHER TYPE
entities.description: A specific phenotype of male sterility characterized by non-separation of tetrads and a persistent tapetum.

entities.name: NON-SEPARATION OF TETRADS
entities.description: A cellular failure in pollen development where tetrads do not separate, seen in translucent anther male sterility.

entities.name: PERSISTENT TAPETUM
entities.description: The continued presence of the tapetum layer in the anther, associated with translucent anther male sterility.

entities.name: MS1
entities.description: A single recessive gene responsible for the translucent anther type of male sterility in pigeonpea.

entities.name: B15B
entities.description: A specific breeding line of pigeonpea exhibiting a distinct male sterile character.

entities.name: B15B MALE STERILE CHARACTER
entities.description: A male sterile phenotype in breeding line B15B characterized by shrivelled, arrowhead-shaped, non-dehiscent, brown-coloured anthers and early tetrad stage degeneration of pollen mother cells.

entities.name: POLLEN MOTHER CELL DEGENERATION
entities.description: The breakdown of pollen mother cells at the early tetrad stage, a characteristic of the B15B male sterile character.
